key_risks: Mega-cap tech re-acceleration (e.g., strong TSMC results) reverses the rotation and lifts cap-weighted SPY above this portfolio
Hot June payrolls or sticky core PCE pushes yields higher, hurting small caps, regional-bank-heavy value, and biotech
Momentum reversal in healthcare/biotech after strong 30-day runs with limited fundamental support in the briefing
Weak Q2 bank earnings or credit deterioration hits the financials overweight

portfolio.rationale: Broad healthcare showing renewed strength with low beta; defensive earnings and sector rotation support continuation with limited drawdown risk.
Rotation into small-cap value is supported by solid labor data, resilient GDP, and steady Fed policy; at 52-week high with moderate volatility.
Q2 bank earnings (JPM, WFC July 14) are a near-term catalyst; sector momentum backed by firm economy and stable rates.
Market breadth is improving while mega-cap tech lags; equal-weight at 52-week high with lower volatility than cap-weighted SPY.
Strong recent breadth (66% up days, small drawdown) alongside healthcare strength; M&A and rate-plateau environment supportive, though momentum reliance is a risk.

portfolio_rationale: Position for continued rotation out of extended mega-cap tech into value, small caps, financials, and healthcare, with bank earnings and a steady Fed as July catalysts.
rationale_summary: Overweight broadening-market beneficiaries (value, small caps, equal-weight, financials, healthcare/biotech) given firm macro data, lagging mega-cap tech, and mid-July bank earnings catalysts.
